_description: Template for your own golden self-test cases. Copy to golden-cases.json and add your validated closed PRs. Commander runs workers against these in dryrun mode and compares output. A regression means a commander prompt change made worker output meaningfully worse.
_how_to_add: After a manual Stage-0 validation of a closed PR in one of your repos, capture: repo, pr_num, parent_commit (pre-fix), key assertions (what the worker MUST produce), and minimum quality signals (tests added? no source regressions? build passes?).

_requires_legend.requires_docker: Skip the case if `docker info` fails (no docker daemon reachable). Used by cases that spin up localstack / test containers.
_requires_legend.requires_linear_mcp: Skip the case if the Linear MCP server isn't installed.
_requires_legend.requires_gh_auth: Skip the case if `gh auth status` fails.
_requires_legend.requires_claude_cli: Skip the case if `claude` CLI isn't on PATH (setup.sh hard-fails without it).
_requires_legend.requires_flyctl: Skip the case if `fly` (flyctl) isn't on PATH. Used by `fly config validate` checks against infra/fly.toml.
_requires_legend.requires_hadolint: Skip the case if `hadolint` isn't on PATH. Used for Dockerfile linting checks.

cases.ticket_body_summary: Short description of what the ticket asked for, as you'd write it if re-opening the issue today

cases._comment: STUB for the retro command (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-retro-workflow.md). Populate `fixture_logs_path`, `fixture_citations_path`, and `expected_sections` with real values once you have a week of real logs. Until then, this stub documents the shape so the next operator knows what a retro golden case looks like. Runs against the commander itself (no worker subagent) — retro is an inline commander procedure, not a spawned worker.
cases.ticket_body_summary: Run `retro` against a fixed snapshot of logs/ + state/memory-citations.json + memory/escalation-faq.md and verify the output matches the template structure in CLAUDE.md (Weekly retro section).

cases._comment: Commander-side fixture — not a PR regression. Validates scheduled autopickup's tick procedure against simulated queue/PAUSE/rate-limit states. Verifies quiet-by-default, yield-to-foreground, and 2-strike rate-limit auto-disable. See docs/specs/2026-04-16-scheduled-autopickup.md and CLAUDE.md 'Scheduled autopickup'.
cases.ticket_body_summary: Operator runs `autopickup every 5 min`. Simulate 3 consecutive ticks under different fixture conditions and verify commander-side behavior.

ticks._comment: Healthy queue; two ready tickets, no active workers, no pause, no rate-limit.
Rate-limit preflight fails — no spawn, increment consecutive_rate_limit_hits, stay quiet (this is the FIRST strike, not yet at the 2-strike threshold).
Second consecutive rate-limit preflight failure — hit the 2-strike auto-disable. enabled flips to false, ONE chat message surfaces, /loop exits.

yield_behavior_check._comment: Separate assertion: if operator types a message during a tick window, the tick must NOT race the foreground turn. Commander handles the operator first, resumes on next scheduled tick.
yield_behavior_check.expected: Tick #2 is deferred; 'status' gets a normal response; tick runs at next interval boundary.

cases._description: Script-only self-test for the default-on autopickup behavior (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-autopickup-default-on.md, ticket #17). Verifies that a fresh setup.sh seeds state/autopickup.json with auto_enable_on_session_start=true, and that the ./hydra launcher's --no-autopickup flag exports HYDRA_NO_AUTOPICKUP=1. Runs locally; no worker spawn, no worktree. setup.sh is run non-interactively with an empty stdin so the external-memory-dir prompt accepts the default. setup.sh hard-fails its prereq check if `claude` is not on PATH, so this case is marked requires_claude_cli — it SKIPs on CI runners where the Claude Code CLI isn't installed.

steps.name: fresh setup.sh seeds state/autopickup.json with auto_enable_on_session_start=true
steps.cmd: rm -f state/autopickup.json && bash setup.sh <<<'' >/dev/null && jq -r .auto_enable_on_session_start state/autopickup.json

steps.name: fresh setup.sh seeds enabled=false (session-start logic flips it, not setup)
HYDRA_NO_AUTOPICKUP env var is visible to the launcher's env

cases._description: Script-only self-test for the non-chat CLI subcommands added in ticket #25 (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-hydra-cli.md). Syntax-checks each new helper, exercises --help, runs list-repos against a deterministic fixture, and round-trips pause/resume. Runs locally; no worker spawn, no chat, no network. Commander runs this before committing changes to any scripts/hydra-*.sh that were introduced for the CLI subcommand set.

steps.name: bash -n passes on every new hydra-*.sh CLI helper
steps.cmd: for s in scripts/hydra-add-repo.sh scripts/hydra-remove-repo.sh scripts/hydra-list-repos.sh scripts/hydra-status.sh scripts/hydra-pause.sh scripts/hydra-resume.sh scripts/hydra-issue.sh; do bash -n "$s" || { echo "FAIL: $s"; exit 1; }; done && echo OK

steps.name: hydra-add-repo.sh refuses a bad slug (exit 1)
steps.cmd: NO_COLOR=1 scripts/hydra-add-repo.sh not-a-slug 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && echo BADSLUG_OK

steps.name: hydra-add-repo.sh refuses a nonexistent local-path (exit 1)
steps.cmd: NO_COLOR=1 scripts/hydra-add-repo.sh foo/bar --local-path /nonexistent/path/for/fixture --non-interactive 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && echo NOPATH_OK

steps.name: hydra-list-repos.sh against fixture emits 3 rows + header
hydra-issue.sh refuses garbage arg (exit 2)
steps.cmd: NO_COLOR=1 scripts/hydra-issue.sh not-a-valid-ref 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 2 ] && echo PARSE_ERR

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/ensure-repo-cloned.sh (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-cloud-repo-clone.md, ticket #73). Uses the public octocat/Hello-World repo so no GITHUB_TOKEN is required in CI; the unauthenticated HTTPS clone path is the same code path the cloud commander takes for public repos. Each step builds a fresh tmpdir fixture, runs the script, and asserts behavior via exit code + shell grep. Guardrails: bad-slug + missing-repo arguments both exit non-zero; first clone produces a .git dir; second run re-fetches without re-cloning (same SHA and same .git/config inode).

steps.name: bad slug (no slash) exits 1 with clear message
slug not in repos.json exits 1
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && printf '{"repos":[]}\n' > "$TMP/repos.json" && HYDRA_REPOS_JSON="$TMP/repos.json" scripts/ensure-repo-cloned.sh doesnotexist/doesnotexist 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && echo MISSING_OK

steps.name: first call clones octocat/Hello-World; second call re-fetches (same SHA, same .git inode)
steps.cmd: set -euo pipefail; TMP=$(mktemp -d); trap 'rm -rf "$TMP"' EXIT; printf '{"repos":[{"owner":"octocat","name":"Hello-World","local_path":"/tmp/unused","enabled":true,"ticket_trigger":"assignee","cloud_local_path":"%s/clone"}]}\n' "$TMP" > "$TMP/repos.json"; export HYDRA_REPOS_JSON="$TMP/repos.json"; export HYDRA_CLONE_DEPTH=5; scripts/ensure-repo-cloned.sh octocat/Hello-World >/dev/null 2>&1; test -d "$TMP/clone/.git" || { echo FAIL_NO_GIT; exit 1; }; sha1=$(git -C "$TMP/clone" rev-parse HEAD); inode1=$(ls -di "$TMP/clone/.git/config" | awk '{print $1}'); scripts/ensure-repo-cloned.sh octocat/Hello-World >/dev/null 2>&1; sha2=$(git -C "$TMP/clone" rev-parse HEAD); inode2=$(ls -di "$TMP/clone/.git/config" | awk '{print $1}'); [ "$sha1" = "$sha2" ] || { echo FAIL_SHA_DIVERGED; exit 1; }; [ "$inode1" = "$inode2" ] || { echo FAIL_INODE_DIVERGED; exit 1; }; echo IDEMPOTENT_OK

steps.name: no GITHUB_TOKEN leaked into .git/config on first clone
steps.cmd:
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d); cat > "$TMPDIR/repos.json" <<CFG
{"repos": [{"owner": "octocat", "name": "Hello-World", "local_path": "/tmp/unused", "enabled": true, "git_url": "https://github.com/octocat/Hello-World.git", "cloud_local_path": "$TMPDIR/clone"}]}
CFG
GITHUB_TOKEN=FAKE_TOKEN_LEAK_SENTINEL HYDRA_REPOS_JSON="$TMPDIR/repos.json" bash scripts/ensure-repo-cloned.sh octocat/Hello-World > /dev/null 2>&1 && ! grep -q FAKE_TOKEN_LEAK_SENTINEL "$TMPDIR/clone/.git/config" && echo NO_TOKEN_LEAK

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-doctor.sh. Exercises the Scripts category in isolation — deterministic, doesn't depend on external state, so it's safe to run in any Hydra worktree. Also asserts --help prints usage. Commander runs this before committing changes to scripts/hydra-doctor.sh. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-hydra-doctor.md (ticket #36).

Script-only self-test: exercises scripts/linear-poll.sh against the mock Linear MCP response under self-test/fixtures/linear/. Runs locally; no Linear MCP or credentials required. Commander runs this before committing changes to scripts/linear-poll.sh, state/linear.example.json, or docs/linear-tool-contract.md. The fixture pins the assumed Linear MCP response shape — if an operator validates that shape against their real Linear MCP install and it differs, the fixture AND the tool contract doc must be updated together (same PR).

steps.name: linear-poll.sh --fixture parses mock response
steps.expected_stdout_contains: OK: linear-poll.sh --fixture parses mock response correctly

cases._description: Script-only self-test: exercises scripts/validate-state.sh and scripts/validate-state-all.sh against fixtures under self-test/fixtures/state-schemas/. Verifies that the minimal bash+jq path accepts valid fixtures and rejects broken ones (one invalid fixture per schema, each breaks a different required top-level key), that the bulk runner aggregates results, and that --strict fails cleanly when ajv-cli isn't installed. Runs locally; no worker spawn, no worktree, no network. Commander runs this before committing changes to scripts/validate-state*.sh, state/schemas/*, or the fixtures themselves. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-state-schemas.md (ticket #30).

steps.name: all state schemas are valid JSON
all valid fixtures are valid JSON
all invalid fixtures are structurally valid JSON (only schema rejects them)
validate-state.sh passes on valid active fixture
validate-state.sh passes on valid budget-used fixture
validate-state.sh passes on valid autopickup fixture
validate-state.sh passes on valid memory-citations fixture
validate-state.sh passes on valid repos fixture
validate-state.sh rejects budget-used fixture missing wall-clock key
validate-state.sh rejects autopickup fixture missing last_run
validate-state.sh rejects memory-citations fixture missing citations
validate-state.sh rejects repos fixture missing repos
validate-state-all.sh exits 0 on valid fixtures dir
validate-state-all.sh exits 1 on invalid fixtures dir
validate-state-all.sh failure summary names every failing file
validate-state.sh --strict without ajv exits 2 with clear message
steps.cmd: if command -v ajv >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo SKIP_HAS_AJV; else scripts/validate-state.sh --strict --schema-dir state/schemas self-test/fixtures/state-schemas/valid/active.json 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 2 ] && echo STRICT_NOAJV_OK; fi

steps.name: validate-state-all.sh --strict without ajv exits 2 with clear message
steps.cmd: if command -v ajv >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo SKIP_HAS_AJV; else scripts/validate-state-all.sh --strict --state-dir self-test/fixtures/state-schemas/valid 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 2 ] && echo STRICT_NOAJV_OK; fi

cases._description: Script-only self-test: exercises scripts/parse-citations.sh, scripts/validate-learning-entry.sh, and scripts/validate-citations.sh against fixtures under self-test/fixtures/memory-validator/. Runs locally; no worker spawn, no worktree. Commander runs this before committing changes to any of those scripts or the memory schema.

steps.name: parse-citations emits citations delta from fixture report
validate-learning-entry fails on fixture missing frontmatter
validate-citations passes when all quotes are live

cases._description: Self-tests the self-test runner. Invokes ./self-test/run.sh against fake-golden-cases.json fixtures (self-test/fixtures/self-test-runner/) — one deliberately passing case, one deliberately failing — and asserts exit codes + error-message shape. Runs before committing changes to self-test/run.sh itself. See docs/specs/2026-04-16-self-test-runner.md.

steps.name: runner failure output names the failing step
steps.cmd: NO_COLOR=1 ./self-test/run.sh --cases-file self-test/fixtures/self-test-runner/fake-golden-cases.json --case fake-fail 2>&1 | grep -F 'second step intentionally fails'

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/release.sh (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-release-process.md, ticket #28). Stages a minimal mirror tree in a tmpdir with fixture VERSION + CHANGELOG.md, invokes release.sh against it, verifies --dry-run does not mutate files and that downgrade / equal-version / non-semver all exit non-zero. Runs before committing changes to scripts/release.sh, VERSION, or CHANGELOG.md.

Script-only self-test for scripts/rescue-worker.sh (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-worker-timeout-watchdog.md, ticket #45). Exercises the non-mutating --probe mode against a throwaway git repo that simulates each rescue state (clean / dirty / ahead-of-main), checks --help / missing-args / branch-mismatch error paths, and verifies bash -n cleanliness. The --rescue mutating path (commit + rebase + push) is exercised by commander on real worker worktrees and by the worker-subagent SKIP stub (worker-timeout-watchdog-rescue) — it can't run in pure script-kind because it needs a live remote.

steps.name: rescue-worker.sh missing mode returns usage error (exit 2)
steps.cmd: NO_COLOR=1 scripts/rescue-worker.sh /tmp hydra/commander/45 45 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 2 ] && echo USAGE_ERR

steps.name: rescue-worker.sh missing positional args returns usage error (exit 2)
rescue-worker.sh --probe on nonexistent worktree exits 1
steps.cmd: NO_COLOR=1 scripts/rescue-worker.sh --probe /nonexistent/path/for/fixture-$$ hydra/commander/45 45 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && echo NOPATH_OK

cases._description: Script-only self-test for the hydra-operator Main Agent skill (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-hydra-operator-skill.md, ticket #53). Verifies that skills/hydra-operator/SKILL.md exists with the required YAML frontmatter fields (name, description, mcpServers) that Claude Code's skill loader needs, that the install README is present and contains the operator-facing `cp -r` install command, and that copying the skill directory into a fake user-scoped skills dir produces a readable SKILL.md. Runs locally; no worker spawn, no worktree. Commander runs this before committing changes to skills/hydra-operator/.

steps.name: install-target SKILL.md exists at the expected path
install README exists and documents the cp -r install command
install smoke — copying into a fake skills dir yields a readable SKILL.md
steps.cmd: tmp=$(mktemp -d) && cp -r skills/hydra-operator "$tmp/" && ls "$tmp/hydra-operator/SKILL.md" && echo OK

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/state-get.sh + scripts/state-put.sh against a localstack-backed DynamoDB (Phase 2 adapter — see docs/specs/2026-04-16-s3-knowledge-store.md and ticket #13). Spins up localstack via docker compose, creates the bucket + commander-* tables, puts a JSON blob via state-put.sh (dynamodb backend, localstack endpoint), reads it back via state-get.sh, compares bit-for-bit, tears down. Marked requires_docker: skipped on machines where `docker info` fails.

steps.name: local-mode smoke — backend unset defaults to local and roundtrips a JSON blob
steps.cmd: tmp=$(mktemp -d) && echo '{"workers":[],"last_updated":null}' > "$tmp/active.json" && scripts/state-get.sh --key active --state-dir "$tmp" && echo '{"workers":[{"id":"w1"}],"last_updated":"2026-04-16T00:00:00Z"}' | scripts/state-put.sh --key active --state-dir "$tmp" && scripts/state-get.sh --key active --state-dir "$tmp"

steps.name: memory-mount.sh --dry-run prints the mount-s3 command when env is set
localstack e2e roundtrip (put via dynamodb backend, get, compare)

cases._description: Exercises worker-conflict-resolver on two PRs that both added rows to the same markdown commands table in CLAUDE.md. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-worker-conflict-resolver.md (ticket #7). SKIPped in the current runner — needs a worker-subagent-kind executor (same path that ticket #15 blocks on). Fixture under self-test/fixtures/conflict-resolver/: base-commands-table.md (starting state, 5 rows), pr-a-diff.patch (adds 'retro'), pr-b-diff.patch (adds 'autopickup'), expected-resolution.md (both rows, no dupes), semantic-conflict-example.md (documented STOP-and-escalate case, for contrast).
cases._skip_reason: requires worker-conflict-resolver spawn against live PRs; implement when worker-subagent SKIP-path executor lands
cases.ticket_body_summary: Two open PRs both add a new row to the same markdown commands table in CLAUDE.md. Commander invokes worker-conflict-resolver to merge them deterministically into one superseding PR.

assertions.expected_artifact: worker opens a draft PR titled 'Merge #A + #B into main' that supersedes both source PRs; resolution contains both added table rows with no duplicates; source PRs are labeled commander-superseded and get a comment linking the new PR

cases._description: Exercises worker-test-discovery end-to-end against a minimal fixture repo (self-test/fixtures/test-discovery/fixture-repo/) that has no TESTING.md, no CLAUDE.md, and no README testing section. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-exercise-test-discovery.md (ticket #32). Today the runner SKIPs this case — it needs the same worker-implementation SKIP-path executor that ticket #15 is about to land. The follow-up ticket (#15-follow-up) is to switch this case from SKIP to live execution once that executor is merged.
cases._skip_reason: requires worker-test-discovery spawn; implement when worker-implementation SKIP path lands
cases.ticket_body_summary: Worker-implementation has asked 'test procedure unclear' twice on this repo. Commander auto-dispatched worker-test-discovery to document how to run + verify the service.

assertions.expected_artifact: worker opens a PR adding TESTING.md to the fixture repo with the docker compose up + curl/redis-cli localhost:6379 verify steps

cases._description: Verifies that a Commander-spawned worker subagent correctly inherits the strict permission denylist from .claude/settings.local.json. Executes self-test/fixtures/permission-probe/probe-script.sh inside a worker subagent, captures JSONL output, diffs against self-test/fixtures/permission-probe/expected-outcomes.json. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-permission-audit.md. Audit doc: docs/security-audit-2026-04-17-permission-inheritance.md. Ticket: #31. Today the runner SKIPs this case — same worker-subagent execution path that test-discovery-dormant-to-active needs (ticket #15 follow-up). Until then, the operator runs the probe manually per the audit doc's 'Manual operator run' section.
cases._skip_reason: requires worker-implementation SKIP-path executor; when that lands, this case becomes live and gives the operator a per-commit receipt on permission-inheritance
cases.ticket_body_summary: Permission inheritance probe. Spawn a worker subagent whose only job is to `bash self-test/fixtures/permission-probe/probe-script.sh --worktree-root "$PWD" --output /tmp/permission-probe-observed.jsonl`, then emit the captured JSONL back to Commander. Commander diffs observed vs expected-outcomes.json; any mismatch is a finding.

cases._description: Simulates a worker-implementation whose chat stream dies mid-ticket (18-minute stream-idle timeout — see ticket #45 observed pattern). The fixture would spawn a worker that writes a partial set of files to a worktree, then sleeps past the 12-minute watchdog threshold without ever returning. Commander detects silence + absent completion log, runs scripts/rescue-worker.sh --probe, sees 'rescue-uncommitted' or 'rescue-commits', runs --rescue, then spawns a fresh worker with a resume hint that picks up from the partial work and opens the PR. Ticket #45. Today the runner SKIPs this case — it needs both the worker-subagent executor (same blocker as test-discovery-dormant-to-active, permission-probe, conflict-resolver-additive) AND a timer-mocking primitive so the 12-min wait doesn't actually take 12 minutes on CI.
cases._skip_reason: requires worker-subagent SKIP-path executor + timer mocking; implement when worker-implementation SKIP path lands (ticket #15 follow-up)
cases.ticket_body_summary: A worker-implementation spawned on a fresh ticket goes silent at the 18-minute stream-idle mark after committing partial work. Commander's watchdog (CLAUDE.md 'Worker timeout watchdog') must detect the silence within 12 minutes, run scripts/rescue-worker.sh --probe, run --rescue if probe returns rescue-commits or rescue-uncommitted, then spawn a follow-up worker that finishes the ticket and opens the PR. A 'nothing-to-rescue' probe labels the ticket commander-stuck for operator retry.

must_include_patterns: rescue: partial work from timed-out worker
assertions.expected_artifact: worker opens a draft PR containing both the partial rescued commits and the completion commits; the ticket has a 'commander-rescued' label and a comment listing the rescue commit SHAs

cases._description: Dry-run of worker-auditor against a fixture worktree seeded with a known drift (CLAUDE.md asserts behavior X, no code supports X). Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-worker-auditor-subagent.md. Ticket: #83. Exercises three guarantees at once: (1) worker physically cannot edit files — after the run, `git status` on the worktree is clean; (2) self-limit holds — even if the fixture contains >3 drifts, the worker files exactly 3; (3) dedup works — if one of the expected findings has a pre-existing open issue whose title substring-matches the candidate, only N-1 issues are filed. SKIPped today, same blocker as the other worker-subagent cases (test-discovery-dormant-to-active, permission-probe, conflict-resolver-additive, worker-timeout-watchdog-rescue) — needs the worker-subagent SKIP-path executor from ticket #15 follow-up. This case documents the expected Q+A shape so the executor has a concrete target when it lands.
cases._skip_reason: requires worker-auditor spawn against a fixture worktree; implement when worker-subagent SKIP-path executor lands (ticket #15 follow-up)
cases.ticket_body_summary: Operator typed `audit`. Commander spawns worker-auditor against the hydra repo. The fixture worktree contains a planted drift: a CLAUDE.md claim with no supporting code, plus two additional drifts that stress the ≤3 cap and dedup logic.

assertions.expected_artifact: worker files between 1 and 3 GitHub issues via `gh issue create`, each labeled commander-ready + commander-auto-filed + tier-<N>, each body rendered from docs/templates/auto-filed-issue.md with all {{PLACEHOLDER}} values substituted. Worktree `git status` is clean after the run — zero files modified. Worker's final report lists the filed issue numbers.

cases._description: Script-only self-test for the runnable 10-minute demo under examples/hello-hydra-demo/ (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-demo-repo.md, ticket #29). Runs the demo's node:test suite, checks gh-filings.sh syntax, and verifies the fixture-issue + DEMO.md shape is intact. Guarantees the demo never silently rots — if somebody breaks the tests, the fixture frontmatter, or the DEMO.md cross-links, commander catches it before committing. Zero external deps: node 18+ is the only requirement.

steps.name: all 3 fixture issue markdown files exist
fixture issue #1 has YAML-ish title/body shape
fixture issue #2 has YAML-ish title/body shape
fixture issue #3 has YAML-ish title/body shape
DEMO.md exists and references all 3 fixture files
steps.cmd: test -f examples/hello-hydra-demo/DEMO.md && grep -q '01-t1-typo.md' examples/hello-hydra-demo/DEMO.md && grep -q '02-t2-farewell.md' examples/hello-hydra-demo/DEMO.md && grep -q '03-t3-rotate-key.md' examples/hello-hydra-demo/DEMO.md && echo OK

steps.name: README intentionally retains the 'hollo' typo (it's the T1 target)
package.json is zero-dep (no dependencies / devDependencies keys with entries)
steps.cmd: jq -e '((.dependencies // {}) | length) == 0 and ((.devDependencies // {}) | length) == 0' examples/hello-hydra-demo/package.json >/dev/null && echo OK

cases._description: Script-only self-test for the MCP tool contract landed in ticket #50 (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-mcp-agent-interface.md). Validates that mcp/hydra-tools.json is well-formed JSON, declares exactly 13 tools, and that every tool entry has all required fields (name, description, scope, input_schema, output_schema) with scope drawn from the documented set {read, spawn, merge, admin}. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-dual-mode-workflow.md (ticket #51).

steps.name: every tool has name, description, scope, input_schema, output_schema
steps.cmd: jq -e '.tools | all(has("name") and has("description") and has("scope") and has("input_schema") and has("output_schema"))' mcp/hydra-tools.json && echo OK

steps.name: every tool.scope is one of {read, spawn, merge, admin}
steps.cmd: jq -e '.tools | all(.scope == "read" or .scope == "spawn" or .scope == "merge" or .scope == "admin")' mcp/hydra-tools.json && echo OK

steps.name: every tool.name starts with 'hydra.'
steps.cmd: jq -e '.tools | all(.name | startswith("hydra."))' mcp/hydra-tools.json && echo OK

cases._description: Script-only self-test for state/connectors/mcp.json.example (landed in ticket #50). Verifies the template is valid JSON and has the three required top-level sections: server, authorized_agents, upstream_supervisor. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-dual-mode-workflow.md (ticket #51).

Script-only self-test for infra/fly.toml (landed in ticket #48). Runs `fly config validate` to catch drift from Fly.io's schema before a deploy breaks. Marked requires_flyctl: SKIPs on machines where the flyctl (`fly`) CLI isn't installed — including CI runners. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-dual-mode-workflow.md (ticket #51).

steps.name: fly config validate passes against infra/fly.toml
steps.cmd: fly config validate --config infra/fly.toml 2>&1 && echo VALIDATED

cases._description: Script-only self-test for infra/Dockerfile (landed in ticket #48). Runs hadolint to catch common Dockerfile anti-patterns (bad ordering, missing --no-install-recommends, unpinned tags, etc). Marked requires_hadolint: SKIPs on machines where hadolint isn't installed. `docker build --check` was considered as a fallback but only exists in recent Docker Buildx — hadolint is the portable option. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-dual-mode-workflow.md (ticket #51).

Script-only self-test for infra/entrypoint.sh (landed in ticket #48). Verifies (1) syntax clean, (2) --help prints usage, (3) entrypoint fails loudly when neither CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN nor ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set. Uses `env -i` to wipe the host's environment so inherited tokens don't mask the guard. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-dual-mode-workflow.md (ticket #51).

steps.name: entrypoint.sh fails loud when no auth env is set
steps.cmd: env -i HOME="$HOME" PATH="$PATH" bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -ne 0 ] && echo FAILED_LOUD

steps.name: entrypoint.sh no-auth error message names CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
steps.cmd: env -i HOME="$HOME" PATH="$PATH" bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1 | grep -F 'CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN'

cases._description: Script-only self-test for entrypoint.sh stanzas 5b (repo-clone loop) and 5c (slack bot launch), landed in ticket #80. Uses HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 to exercise the conditional logic without actually cloning or launching anything. Three scenarios via fixture dirs: (1) enabled repo + missing slack.json → logs would-clone + slack-skip; (2) slack.json with enabled=true → logs would-launch-bot; (3) slack.json with enabled=false → logs slack-skip. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-entrypoint-integration.md.

steps.name: dry-run: enabled repo + no slack.json → logs would-clone
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$TMP/state" && printf '{"repos":[{"owner":"your-org","name":"enabled-repo","enabled":true},{"owner":"your-org","name":"disabled-repo","enabled":false}]}\n' > "$TMP/state/repos.json" && out=$(HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 HYDRA_ROOT="$TMP" CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=stub GITHUB_TOKEN=stub HYDRA_MCP_ENABLED=0 HYDRA_DATA_DIR="$TMP/data" HYDRA_HEALTH_PORT=0 bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1) && rm -rf "$TMP" && echo "$out" | grep -F 'DRY RUN: would clone your-org/enabled-repo' && echo FOUND_CLONE

steps.name: dry-run: disabled repo entries are skipped
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$TMP/state" && printf '{"repos":[{"owner":"your-org","name":"enabled-repo","enabled":true},{"owner":"your-org","name":"disabled-repo","enabled":false}]}\n' > "$TMP/state/repos.json" && out=$(HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 HYDRA_ROOT="$TMP" CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=stub GITHUB_TOKEN=stub HYDRA_MCP_ENABLED=0 HYDRA_DATA_DIR="$TMP/data" HYDRA_HEALTH_PORT=0 bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1) && rm -rf "$TMP" && ( echo "$out" | grep -qF 'disabled-repo' && echo BAD_DISABLED_LEAKED || echo DISABLED_SKIPPED )

steps.name: dry-run: missing slack.json → logs slack skip
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$TMP/state" && printf '{"repos":[]}\n' > "$TMP/state/repos.json" && out=$(HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 HYDRA_ROOT="$TMP" CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=stub GITHUB_TOKEN=stub HYDRA_MCP_ENABLED=0 HYDRA_DATA_DIR="$TMP/data" HYDRA_HEALTH_PORT=0 bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1) && rm -rf "$TMP" && echo "$out" | grep -F 'Slack bot:' | grep -F 'skipping' && echo FOUND_SLACK_SKIP

steps.name: dry-run: slack.json enabled=true → logs would-launch
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$TMP/state/connectors" && printf '{"repos":[]}\n' > "$TMP/state/repos.json" && printf '{"enabled":true}\n' > "$TMP/state/connectors/slack.json" && out=$(HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 HYDRA_ROOT="$TMP" CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=stub GITHUB_TOKEN=stub HYDRA_MCP_ENABLED=0 HYDRA_DATA_DIR="$TMP/data" HYDRA_HEALTH_PORT=0 bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1) && rm -rf "$TMP" && echo "$out" | grep -F 'DRY RUN: would launch hydra-slack-bot' && echo FOUND_SLACK_LAUNCH

steps.name: dry-run: slack.json enabled=false → logs skip (not launch)
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$TMP/state/connectors" && printf '{"repos":[]}\n' > "$TMP/state/repos.json" && printf '{"enabled":false}\n' > "$TMP/state/connectors/slack.json" && out=$(HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 HYDRA_ROOT="$TMP" CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=stub GITHUB_TOKEN=stub HYDRA_MCP_ENABLED=0 HYDRA_DATA_DIR="$TMP/data" HYDRA_HEALTH_PORT=0 bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1) && rm -rf "$TMP" && ( echo "$out" | grep -qF 'would launch hydra-slack-bot' && echo BAD_LAUNCH_LEAKED || echo SLACK_DISABLED_SKIPPED )

steps.name: dry-run: exits before tmux with clean status
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$TMP/state" && printf '{"repos":[]}\n' > "$TMP/state/repos.json" && HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 HYDRA_ROOT="$TMP" CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=stub GITHUB_TOKEN=stub HYDRA_MCP_ENABLED=0 HYDRA_DATA_DIR="$TMP/data" HYDRA_HEALTH_PORT=0 bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1 | grep -F 'HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 — exiting' && rm -rf "$TMP" && echo DRY_RUN_EXIT_LOGGED

cases._description: Script-only self-test for the Slack bot config template (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-slack-bot.md, ticket #74). jq-validates that state/connectors/slack.json.example declares every field bin/hydra-slack-bot reads at startup: enabled (default false), token-env-var pointers, single allowed_user_id, default_channel, poll interval, repos array, seen-PRs cache path. Also smoke-tests the launcher's refuse-to-start paths: missing config, disabled config, and missing env-var tokens — each must exit non-zero (refuse) or zero with a clean log (disabled). No live Slack API test — that would require a real workspace. Runs locally; no worker spawn, no worktree.

steps.name: slack.json.example declares allowed_user_id starting with U
slack.json.example declares repos as an array of owner/name strings
steps.cmd: jq -e '.repos | type == "array" and (length > 0) and all(test("/"))' state/connectors/slack.json.example >/dev/null && echo OK

steps.name: bin/hydra-slack-bot refuses to start on missing config
steps.cmd: tmp=$(mktemp -d) && cp bin/hydra-slack-bot "$tmp/" && mkdir -p "$tmp/bin" && mv "$tmp/hydra-slack-bot" "$tmp/bin/" && cd "$tmp" && ./bin/hydra-slack-bot 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && echo REFUSED_NO_CONFIG

steps.name: bin/hydra-slack-bot exits 0 when enabled=false
steps.cmd: tmp=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$tmp/bin" "$tmp/state/connectors" && cp bin/hydra-slack-bot "$tmp/bin/" && echo '{"enabled":false,"bot_token_env":"X","app_token_env":"Y","allowed_user_id":"U1","default_channel":"D1","notification_poll_interval_sec":60,"repos":["a/b"]}' > "$tmp/state/connectors/slack.json" && cd "$tmp" && ./bin/hydra-slack-bot 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 0 ] && echo DISABLED_CLEAN

steps.name: bin/hydra-slack-bot refuses to start when env tokens missing
steps.cmd: tmp=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$tmp/bin" "$tmp/state/connectors" && cp bin/hydra-slack-bot "$tmp/bin/" && echo '{"enabled":true,"bot_token_env":"HYDRA_SELFTEST_MISSING_BOT","app_token_env":"HYDRA_SELFTEST_MISSING_APP","allowed_user_id":"U1","default_channel":"D1","notification_poll_interval_sec":60,"repos":["a/b"]}' > "$tmp/state/connectors/slack.json" && cd "$tmp" && ./bin/hydra-slack-bot 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && echo REFUSED_NO_TOKENS

steps.name: bin/hydra-slack-bot marks PHASE1-FALLBACK swap points for post-#72

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-mcp-server.py — the agent-only interface server binary landed in ticket #72. Spins the server up in a subprocess on a random free port against a fixture mcp.json, exercises auth (valid token, bad token, scope mismatch), dispatches to implemented tools (get_status, pause), verifies t3-refused on merge_pr, verifies NotImplemented on a scaffolded tool (resume), and asserts the audit log received one line per call. Smoke-test fixture lives at self-test/fixtures/mcp-server/run.sh — same pattern as linear-mcp-contract and release-script-smoke. Runs locally in <5s; no worker spawn, no network, no external auth. Commander runs this before committing changes to scripts/hydra-mcp-server.py, scripts/hydra-mcp-serve.sh, or the launcher dispatch in setup.sh.

steps.name: --check refuses with no config (exit 1)
steps.cmd: HYDRA_MCP_CONFIG=/nonexistent/mcp.json python3 scripts/hydra-mcp-server.py --check 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && echo NOCFG_OK

steps.name: --transport stdio is a Phase 1 non-goal (exit 2)
steps.cmd: python3 scripts/hydra-mcp-server.py --transport stdio 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 2 ] && echo STDIO_REFUSED

steps.name: integration smoke (start server, run 7 calls, assert audit + shapes)
mcp/hydra-tools.json is valid JSON + has 13 tools
every tool in the contract has a handler registered in the server
steps.cmd: jq -r '.tools[].name' mcp/hydra-tools.json | sort > /tmp/contract-tools-$$ && grep -oE 'hydra\.[a-z_]+' scripts/hydra-mcp-server.py | grep -v 'hydra\.' | head -0; grep -oE '"hydra\.[a-z_]+"' scripts/hydra-mcp-server.py | tr -d '"' | sort -u > /tmp/server-tools-$$ && diff /tmp/contract-tools-$$ /tmp/server-tools-$$ && echo HANDLERS_COMPLETE; rm -f /tmp/contract-tools-$$ /tmp/server-tools-$$

cases._description: Fixture case for CLAUDE.md's Scheduled autopickup tick step 1.5 (retro due check). Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-scheduled-retro.md / ticket #85. Exercises four clock scenarios against a fresh state/autopickup.json fixture: (1) last_retro_run=null + Monday 09:05 local fires the retro, (2) last_retro_run=today T09:00:00 + Monday 10:00 local stays silent (idempotent intra-window), (3) last_retro_run=7 days ago + Monday 09:05 local fires (weekly cadence), (4) Monday 08:59 local or Sunday 23:59 local stays silent (time gate). The scheduled-retro logic lives as an inline commander procedure, not as a spawnable helper, so this case needs both a commander-side tick executor AND a clock-mocking primitive (HYDRA_FAKE_NOW=<ISO8601> override) before it can run live. Today it SKIPs under the 'commander-self' kind (matches autopickup-tick-behavior's pattern) but documents the expected behavior for the next executor pass — same pattern as retro-golden-stub, autopickup-tick-behavior, and worker-timeout-watchdog-rescue.
cases._skip_reason: requires commander orchestration + HYDRA_FAKE_NOW clock-mocking primitive; implement when the inline tick executor lands (ticket #85 follow-up)
cases.ticket_body_summary: Each autopickup tick runs a retro-due check: Monday >= 09:00 local AND last_retro_run < today T09:00 -> fire retro, write last_retro_run = now, surface one chat line. Otherwise no-op. Four clock scenarios exercise all branches; the fifth asserts a manual retro in the same Monday window correctly suppresses the scheduled fire.

scenarios.name: manual-retro-earlier-suppresses-scheduled-fire
scenarios._comment: Simulates the manual + scheduled interplay. Operator typed `retro` at 08:30 local (before the 09:00 gate). Manual path wrote last_retro_run = 2026-04-20T08:30:00. When the 09:05 scheduled tick arrives, the idempotency guard reads last_retro_run >= 2026-04-20T09:00:00? No — 08:30 is LESS than 09:00. The fixture therefore MUST still fire, because an early-morning manual run doesn't satisfy the Monday-09:00 semantic. This intentionally captures that the guard is 'after 09:00 today,' not 'any time today.'

assertions.expected_artifact_per_fire: memory/retros/YYYY-WW.md written AND state/autopickup.json:last_retro_run updated to the fake_now_local ISO-8601 string

cases._description: Opt-in self-test for the cloud-worker DinD image variant (ticket #77). SKIPs by default. To run: set HYDRA_TEST_CLOUD_DOCKER=1 on a machine with docker + --privileged support (local laptop with docker desktop works; CI runners generally do NOT). When active, the test (1) builds the image with --build-arg ENABLE_DOCKER=true, (2) confirms docker binary is present + hydra user is in the docker group, (3) runs `docker info` inside the built image under --privileged to verify the inner daemon starts and talks to its socket. Each build is ~400-600 MB + ~60-90s wall-clock, so gating this behind an env var keeps the default CI run fast and prevents false-greens on hosts that can't meaningfully run it. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-cloud-worker-docker.md.

steps.name: build the DinD image variant
steps.cmd: docker build --build-arg ENABLE_DOCKER=true -t hydra-dind-selftest:ci -f infra/Dockerfile . >/tmp/hydra-dind-build-$$.log 2>&1 && echo BUILT_OK || (tail -60 /tmp/hydra-dind-build-$$.log >&2; rm -f /tmp/hydra-dind-build-$$.log; exit 1)

steps.name: docker CLI present in DinD image
steps.cmd: docker run --rm hydra-dind-selftest:ci docker --version | grep -qE 'Docker version' && echo DOCKER_CLI_PRESENT

steps.name: hydra user is in docker group
steps.cmd: docker run --rm hydra-dind-selftest:ci id hydra | grep -qE '\b(docker)\b' && echo HYDRA_IN_DOCKER_GROUP

steps.name: inner dockerd reports a live Server Version under --privileged
steps.cmd: docker run --privileged --rm hydra-dind-selftest:ci sh -c 'dockerd >/tmp/dind.log 2>&1 & for i in $(seq 1 20); do docker info >/tmp/info.log 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done; grep -qE "^\s*Server Version:\s+[0-9]+\." /tmp/info.log && echo DIND_SERVER_UP || (tail -40 /tmp/dind.log /tmp/info.log >&2; exit 1)'

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-mcp-register-agent.sh — the CLI that generates MCP bearer tokens, stores their SHA-256 hash in state/connectors/mcp.json, and prints the raw token once (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-mcp-register-agent.md, ticket #84). Security-adjacent: asserts (a) exit 0 on first-use, (b) stdout token is 43+ chars base64, (c) SHA-256 of the printed token matches the hash in the written config, (d) --rotate produces a different token, preserves scope, keeps a single entry, (e) a non-rotate duplicate call exits 1 with NO config writes (atomicity invariant), plus config-missing auto-bootstrap from mcp.json.example. Fixture lives at self-test/fixtures/mcp-register-agent/run.sh — pattern matches mcp-server-starts. Runs locally in <1s; tmpdir-isolated (HYDRA_MCP_REGISTER_CONFIG points at the tmp path; the real state/connectors/mcp.json is never touched). Commander runs this before committing changes to scripts/hydra-mcp-register-agent.sh or the launcher dispatch in setup.sh.

steps.name: integration smoke (token generation, SHA-256 match, --rotate, duplicate-refusal, bootstrap)
dispatch wired: setup.sh launcher heredoc routes `./hydra mcp register-agent` to the new script
steps.cmd: grep -q 'register-agent) shift; hydra_exec_helper scripts/hydra-mcp-register-agent.sh' setup.sh && echo DISPATCH_WIRED

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py — the outbound MCP client landed in ticket #87. Verifies the 'not-configured' fall-through branch: when state/connectors/mcp.json.example is read (upstream_supervisor.enabled=false by default) OR when the config file is missing entirely, the script exits 2 with a 'not-configured' marker on stdout. Commander reads exit 2 as 'keep legacy commander-stuck behavior, no upstream call made'. Also validates Python syntax, --help output, and that the exit-2 branch never touches the network (no token env var needed to trigger it). Runs locally in <1s; no network, no fixture supervisor required. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-supervisor-escalate-client.md.

steps.name: missing config file → exit 2 + not-configured marker
steps.cmd: out=$(echo '{"worker_id":"w-t","question":"q?"}' | python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --config /nonexistent/mcp.json 2>&1); ec=$?; echo "$out" | grep -q 'not-configured' && [ "$ec" -eq 2 ] && echo DISABLED_OK

steps.name: mcp.json.example (enabled=false) → exit 2 + not-configured marker
steps.cmd: out=$(echo '{"worker_id":"w-t","question":"q?"}' | python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --config state/connectors/mcp.json.example 2>&1); ec=$?; echo "$out" | grep -q 'not-configured' && [ "$ec" -eq 2 ] && echo EXAMPLE_DISABLED_OK

steps.name: exit 2 path writes JSON-parseable stdout
steps.cmd: echo '{"worker_id":"w-t","question":"q?"}' | python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --config state/connectors/mcp.json.example 2>/dev/null | jq -e '.error == "not-configured"' >/dev/null && echo JSON_OK

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py envelope + config validation. Covers the enabled=true branch without making a real network call: uses --check to exercise config parsing, scope enforcement, and envelope shape validation. Verifies (a) valid envelope + valid config + token set → exit 0 with check-ok marker; (b) missing required envelope field → exit 4 + stderr marker; (c) token env var unset → exit 1 + token-missing marker; (d) scope_granted over-broad (anything beyond exactly ['resolve_question']) → exit 1 + scope-overreach marker. All steps stage a fixture mcp.json in a tmpdir — no mutation of the real state/ tree. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-supervisor-escalate-client.md. Related envelope spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-mcp-agent-interface.md.

steps.name: --check with valid config + token + envelope → exit 0 + check-ok
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; printf '%s' '{"upstream_supervisor":{"enabled":true,"transport":"http","connect_url":"http://127.0.0.1:9999/mcp","auth_token_env":"TEST_SUP_TOKEN","scope_granted":["resolve_question"],"timeout_sec":5}}' > "$TMP/mcp.json"; out=$(echo '{"worker_id":"w-1","question":"q?"}' | TEST_SUP_TOKEN=x python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --check --config "$TMP/mcp.json" 2>&1); ec=$?; echo "$out" | grep -q 'check-ok' && [ "$ec" -eq 0 ] && echo CHECK_OK

steps.name: --check with malformed envelope (missing worker_id) → exit 4
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; printf '%s' '{"upstream_supervisor":{"enabled":true,"transport":"http","connect_url":"http://127.0.0.1:9999/mcp","auth_token_env":"TEST_SUP_TOKEN","scope_granted":["resolve_question"],"timeout_sec":5}}' > "$TMP/mcp.json"; err=$(echo '{"question":"missing worker_id"}' | TEST_SUP_TOKEN=x python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --check --config "$TMP/mcp.json" 2>&1 >/dev/null); ec=$?; echo "$err" | grep -q 'missing required field' && [ "$ec" -eq 4 ] && echo SHAPE_REJECTED

steps.name: enabled config + no token env var → exit 1 + token-missing
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; printf '%s' '{"upstream_supervisor":{"enabled":true,"transport":"http","connect_url":"http://127.0.0.1:9999/mcp","auth_token_env":"TEST_SUP_TOKEN_ABSENT_XYZ","scope_granted":["resolve_question"],"timeout_sec":5}}' > "$TMP/mcp.json"; unset TEST_SUP_TOKEN_ABSENT_XYZ; out=$(echo '{"worker_id":"w-1","question":"q?"}' | python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --config "$TMP/mcp.json" 2>&1); ec=$?; echo "$out" | grep -q 'token-missing' && [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && echo TOKEN_MISSING_OK

steps.name: scope_granted over-broad → exit 1 + scope-overreach
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; printf '%s' '{"upstream_supervisor":{"enabled":true,"transport":"http","connect_url":"http://127.0.0.1:9999/mcp","auth_token_env":"TEST_SUP_TOKEN","scope_granted":["resolve_question","admin"],"timeout_sec":5}}' > "$TMP/mcp.json"; out=$(echo '{"worker_id":"w-1","question":"q?"}' | TEST_SUP_TOKEN=x python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --config "$TMP/mcp.json" 2>&1); ec=$?; echo "$out" | grep -q 'scope-overreach' && [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && echo SCOPE_REJECTED

steps.name: unsupported transport (stdio) → exit 1 + unsupported-transport
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; printf '%s' '{"upstream_supervisor":{"enabled":true,"transport":"stdio","connect_url":"/usr/bin/true","auth_token_env":"TEST_SUP_TOKEN","scope_granted":["resolve_question"],"timeout_sec":5}}' > "$TMP/mcp.json"; out=$(echo '{"worker_id":"w-1","question":"q?"}' | TEST_SUP_TOKEN=x python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --config "$TMP/mcp.json" 2>&1); ec=$?; echo "$out" | grep -q 'unsupported-transport' && [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && echo STDIO_REJECTED

cases._description: Script-only self-test for the asciinema demo artifact shipped in ticket #37 (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-asciinema-demo.md). Verifies (a) scripts/record-demo.sh is syntactically valid bash and responds to --help; (b) scripts/record-demo.sh --dry-run exits 0 without launching asciinema; (c) docs/demo/demo.cast exists and its first line is valid asciinema v2 JSON (.version == 2); (d) every subsequent line parses as a 3-element JSON array [timestamp, event_type, data]; (e) when the header .title contains the string 'placeholder' (case-insensitive), the stricter event-count check SKIPs — placeholder casts are intentionally minimal, and failing CI on a placeholder branch would block every other PR until someone re-records. Once an operator ships a real cast (title without 'placeholder'), the event-count floor kicks in automatically.

steps.name: scripts/record-demo.sh --dry-run --no-preflight exits 0 without starting asciinema
steps.cmd: NO_COLOR=1 scripts/record-demo.sh --dry-run --no-preflight > /tmp/hydra-demo-dry.out 2>&1 && grep -q 'dry-run complete' /tmp/hydra-demo-dry.out && ! grep -q 'starting asciinema rec' /tmp/hydra-demo-dry.out && echo DRY_RUN_OK

steps.name: first line of docs/demo/demo.cast is valid asciinema v2 header
steps.cmd: head -1 docs/demo/demo.cast | jq -e '.version == 2 and (.width | type) == "number" and (.height | type) == "number"' > /dev/null && echo HEADER_OK

steps.name: every event line in docs/demo/demo.cast parses as [number, string, string]
steps.cmd: total=$(wc -l < docs/demo/demo.cast); bad=0; for i in $(seq 2 $total); do sed -n "${i}p" docs/demo/demo.cast | jq -e 'type == "array" and length == 3 and (.[0]|type) == "number" and (.[1]|type) == "string" and (.[2]|type) == "string"' > /dev/null || bad=1; done; [ $bad -eq 0 ] && echo EVENTS_OK

steps.name: stricter content checks: SKIP on placeholder, enforce min-event-count on real casts
steps.cmd: if head -1 docs/demo/demo.cast | jq -e '.title // "" | test("placeholder"; "i")' > /dev/null; then echo 'PLACEHOLDER_SKIP (ok — skipping event-count floor)'; else events=$(($(wc -l < docs/demo/demo.cast) - 1)); [ "$events" -ge 20 ] && echo 'REAL_CAST_OK' || { echo "FAIL: real cast has only $events events, need >= 20" >&2; exit 1; }; fi
